_doc: P2 method-D scoping measurements (M1 prefill breakdown, M2 concurrency x prune-rate). Run via scripts/queue (entries appended to configs/queue.json) OR directly. GPU-settle sleep 30s between back-to-back vLLM jobs to avoid stale-process OOM. Total budget ~3.5 GPU·h (under 6 GPU·h escalation line). Env: /home/dell/miniconda3/envs/vtc_serve/bin/python (vLLM 0.10.2, V0).

m1_prefill_breakdown.purpose: Isolate vision-tower forward time from LLM prefill. Decision: vt_frac > 0.30 -> mid-encoder prune worth surgery; < 0.10 -> skip.
m1_prefill_breakdown.cmd: /home/dell/miniconda3/envs/vtc_serve/bin/python -m src.serve_bench --model runs/models/llava-1.5-7b-hf --pruning-rate 0.0 --benchmark gqa --subset eval/subsets/gqa_200.jsonl --metrics-out runs/p2_d/m1_r0_n100.json --max-tokens 1 --max-model-len 4096 --gpu-memory-utilization 0.85 --seed 0 --limit 100 --selector proxy
m1_prefill_breakdown.note: max-tokens=1 makes TTFT ~= pure prefill (no decode). Serial mode (default) for per-request TTFT + vision-tower timing. r0 (no pruning) = the fixed-cost reference.

m2_matrix.purpose: req/s of {r0,r50,r75} at concurrency {1,12}. Decision: if speedup_at_12 > speedup_at_1, load-adaptive budget justified.
m2_matrix.cmd_template: /home/dell/miniconda3/envs/vtc_serve/bin/python -m src.serve_bench --model runs/models/llava-1.5-7b-hf --pruning-rate {pruning} --benchmark gqa --subset eval/subsets/gqa_200.jsonl --metrics-out runs/p2_d/{name}.json --max-tokens 16 --max-model-len 4096 --gpu-memory-utilization 0.85 --seed 0 --limit 100 --selector proxy --max-num-seqs {max_num_seqs} --batch-submit
m2_matrix.note: --batch-submit submits all 100 in one llm.chat() so max_num_seqs engages continuous batching. c1 = no batching (isolates per-request latency); c12 = full batching (KV-pressure-bound throughput). max-tokens=16 = realistic short-answer decode.
